1	# DATABASE SCHEMA (Postgres / Supabase)
2	
3	This document is the single source of truth for tables, columns, constraints, indexes, and visibility rules.
4	
5	Key principle:
6	- The DB must enforce correctness (especially timestamps and required fields) so the system fails loudly instead of silently drifting.
7	
8	> **Triggers and functions** are documented in `docs/INFRASTRUCTURE.md`.
9	
10	---
11	## Timestamp Audit (Recommended)
12	Store the timestamps the worker observed on disk during ingest:
13	- `modified_at` and `file_created_at` (already required)
14	Additionally recommended fields for audit/debug:
15	- `last_stat_observed_at` (when the worker last checked timestamps)
16	- `original_mtime_at_processing` / `original_birthtime_at_processing` (optional)
17	If a timestamp mutation incident occurs, record it in a `worker_incidents` table or an `asset_processing_events` log.
18	
19	
20	## 1) Enums (Create First)
21	- `file_type`: `psd`, `ai`
22	- `asset_status`: `pending`, `processing`, `tagged`, `error`
23	- `queue_status`: `pending`, `claimed`, `processing`, `completed`, `failed`
24	- `asset_type`: `art_piece`, `product`
25	- `art_source`: `freelancer`, `straight_style_guide`, `style_guide_composition`
26	- `workflow_status`:
27	  `product_ideas`, `concept_approved`, `in_development`, `freelancer_art`,
28	  `discontinued`, `in_process`, `customer_adopted`, `licensor_approved`, `other`
29	- `app_name`: `popdam`, `styleguides` — used by `app_access` and `invitations.apps`
30	- `app_role`: `admin`, `user`
31	
32	---
33	
34	## 2) Core Tables
35	
36	### 2.1 licensors
37	- `id uuid PK`
38	- `name text NOT NULL`
39	- `external_id text UNIQUE NULL`
40	- `created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`
41	- `updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`
42	
43	### 2.2 properties
44	- `id uuid PK`
45	- `licensor_id uuid FK NOT NULL`
46	- `name text NOT NULL`
47	- `external_id text UNIQUE NULL`
48	- timestamps
49	
50	### 2.3 characters
51	- `id uuid PK`
52	- `property_id uuid FK NOT NULL`
53	- `name text NOT NULL`
54	- `external_id text UNIQUE NULL`
55	- timestamps
56	
57	### 2.4 product_categories / product_types / product_subtypes
58	As in the build spec (taxonomy tables with optional external_id).
59	
60	### 2.5 assets (main)
61	Canonical path storage uses `relative_path` (see PATH_UTILS.md).
62	
63	Required columns:
64	- `id uuid PK`
65	- `filename text NOT NULL`
66	- `relative_path text NOT NULL`  (canonical, POSIX relative)
67	- `file_type file_type NOT NULL`
68	- `file_size bigint DEFAULT 0`
69	- `width int DEFAULT 0`
70	- `height int DEFAULT 0`
71	- `artboards int DEFAULT 1`
72	- `thumbnail_url text NULL` (full public URL)
73	- `thumbnail_error text NULL`
74	- `is_licensed boolean DEFAULT false`
75	- `licensor_id uuid FK NULL`
76	- `property_id uuid FK NULL`
77	- `product_subtype_id uuid FK NULL`
78	- `asset_type asset_type NULL`
79	- `art_source art_source NULL`
80	- `big_theme text NULL`
81	- `little_theme text NULL`
82	- `design_ref text NULL`
83	- `design_style text NULL`
84	- `ai_description text NULL`
85	- `scene_description text NULL`
86	- `tags text[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::text[]`
87	- `workflow_status workflow_status DEFAULT 'other'`
88	- `status asset_status DEFAULT 'pending'`
89	- Visibility Guard: Add is_deleted boolean DEFAULT false.
90	- Integrity Guard: Add UNIQUE(share_id, relative_path) to the assets table to prevent duplicate ingestion.
91	
92	Hashing + scan bookkeeping:
93	- `quick_hash text NOT NULL`
94	- `quick_hash_version int NOT NULL DEFAULT 1`
95	- `last_seen_at timestamptz NOT NULL`
96	- `modified_at timestamptz NOT NULL`  **NO DEFAULT** (must be supplied by agent from disk)
97	- `file_created_at timestamptz NULL`  (agent supplies birthtime if available else = modified_at)
98	- `ingested_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`
99	- `created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`
100	
101	Designer metadata (extracted by AI from Tech Pack thumbnails):
102	- `designer_name text NULL`
103	- `technical_designer_name text NULL`
104	- `freelancer_name text NULL`
105	
106	Hard constraints:
107	- `modified_at` must be NOT NULL and have no default.
108	- `relative_path` must be NOT NULL.
109	
110	### 2.6 asset_characters (join table)
111	- `asset_id uuid FK ON DELETE CASCADE`
112	- `character_id uuid FK`
113	- `UNIQUE(asset_id, character_id)`
114	
115	### 2.7 asset_path_history
116	- `id uuid PK`
117	- `asset_id uuid FK`
118	- `old_relative_path text`
119	- `new_relative_path text`
120	- `detected_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`
121	
122	### 2.8 processing_queue
123	- `id uuid PK`
124	- `asset_id uuid FK`
125	- `job_type text`
126	- `status queue_status DEFAULT 'pending'`
127	- `agent_id text NULL`
128	- `claimed_at timestamptz NULL`
129	- `completed_at timestamptz NULL`
130	- `error_message text NULL`
131	
132	### 2.9 render_queue (optional windows render)
133	- `id uuid PK`
134	- `asset_id uuid FK`
135	- `status queue_status DEFAULT 'pending'`
136	- `claimed_by text NULL`
137	- `claimed_at timestamptz NULL`
138	- `completed_at timestamptz NULL`
139	- `error_message text NULL`
140	
141	### 2.10 style_groups
142	- `id uuid PK`
143	- `sku text NOT NULL`
144	- `folder_path text NOT NULL`
145	- `primary_asset_id uuid FK NULL`
146	- `primary_asset_type text NULL` (mirrors selected primary asset `asset_type` for direct filtering)
147	- `asset_count int DEFAULT 0`
148	- `latest_file_date timestamptz NULL`
149	- `workflow_status workflow_status DEFAULT 'other'`
150	- licensing + taxonomy summary fields (licensor/property/category/division/MG/size)
151	- Designer rollup (from member assets, with conflict detection):
152	  - `designer_name text NULL`
153	  - `technical_designer_name text NULL`
154	  - `freelancer_name text NULL`
155	  - `designer_conflict boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false` (true when member assets have differing designer names)
156	
157	### 2.11 agent_registrations
158	- `id uuid PK`
159	- `agent_name text NOT NULL`
160	- `agent_key_hash text UNIQUE NOT NULL`
161	- `last_heartbeat timestamptz`
162	- `metadata jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'`
163	
164	### 2.12 profiles / user_roles / invitations
165	Invitation-only access model:
166	- profiles: `user_id uuid UNIQUE`, `email text`, `full_name text`, timestamps
167	- user_roles: `user_id uuid`, `role text`, `UNIQUE(user_id, role)`
168	- invitations: `id uuid PK`, `email text UNIQUE NOT NULL`, `role text DEFAULT 'user'`, `invited_by uuid NULL`, `created_at`, `accepted_at NULL`
169	
170	### 2.13 admin_config
171	- `key text PK`
172	- `value jsonb NOT NULL`
173	- `updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`
174	- `updated_by uuid NULL`
175	
176	Stores:
177	- `THUMBNAIL_MIN_DATE`
178	- `SCAN_MIN_DATE`
179	- `SPACES_CONFIG` — DigitalOcean Spaces non-secret config (bucket, region, endpoint, public_base_url)
180	- `BULK_OPERATIONS` — state for all bulk jobs (see `docs/BULK_JOBS.md`)
181	- `SCAN_REQUEST` — pending scan trigger flags for agents
182	- `ERP_LAST_SYNC_DATE` — watermark for incremental ERP sync
183	- `ERP_SYNC_ENDPOINT` — override ERP API URL
184	- `ERP_CATEGORY_CUTOFF_DATE` — items before this date have mg_category nulled (legacy)
185	- taxonomy endpoint URLs
186	- NAS mapping keys (host/ip/share/mount root)
187	- AI tagging custom instructions
188	
189	### 2.14 ERP Tables
190	
191	#### `erp_sync_runs`
192	Metadata per sync execution.
193	- `id uuid PK`, `status text` (running/completed/failed), `started_at`, `ended_at`
194	- `total_fetched int`, `total_upserted int`, `total_errors int`, `error_samples jsonb`
195	- `run_metadata jsonb` (sync_mode, start_date, end_date), `created_by text`
196	
197	#### `erp_items_current`
198	Latest normalized row per ERP item. Upserted on each sync.
199	- `id uuid PK`, `external_id text UNIQUE NOT NULL`
200	- `style_number text`, `item_description text`
201	- `mg_category text` — category from ERP API ("Wall", "Storage", etc.) — may be null
202	- `mg01_code text`, `mg02_code text`, `mg03_code text` — **single-character letter/digit codes** resolved from the API
203	- `mg04_code`, `mg05_code`, `mg06_code` — additional MG levels (rarely populated)
204	- `size_code`, `licensor_code`, `property_code`, `division_code`, `prepack_code`, `prepack_codes text[]`
205	- `erp_updated_at timestamptz` — ERP's own last-modified (used as "Created Date" in the browser)
206	- `synced_at timestamptz`, `sync_run_id uuid FK erp_sync_runs`
207	- `raw_mg_fields jsonb` — original API values: `mg01`/`mg02`/`mg03` (may be descriptions or codes), plus `mg01_code`/`mg02_code`/`mg03_code` (resolved) and other metadata fields
208	- `dismissed boolean DEFAULT false` — soft-hide from browser
209	
210	#### `erp_items_raw`
211	Immutable snapshot of every ERP API response row.
212	- `id uuid PK`, `external_id text`, `raw_payload jsonb`, `sync_run_id uuid FK`, `fetched_at timestamptz`
213	
214	#### `product_category_predictions`
215	AI classification results for ERP items missing `mg_category`.
216	- `id uuid PK`, `external_id text`, `erp_item_id uuid FK erp_items_current`
217	- `predicted_category text`, `confidence real`, `rationale text`
218	- `classification_source text` (erp/rule/ai), `ai_model text`
219	- `status text` (pending/auto_applied/approved/rejected/unclassifiable)
220	- `reviewed_by uuid`, `reviewed_at timestamptz`, `input_context jsonb`
221	
222	### 2.15 Hygiene & Style Guide Tables
223	
224	#### `hygiene_findings`
225	File naming/structure issues found during Windows Agent scans.
226	- `id uuid PK`, `scan_session_id uuid`, `file_path text`, `finding_type text`, `detail jsonb`, `found_at timestamptz`
227	
228	#### `style_guide_crawl_runs`
229	Metadata for each licensor style guide crawl run.
230	- `id uuid PK`, `status text`, `agent_id text`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, `files_found int`, `error_message text`, `inaccessible_roots text[] NULL`
231	
232	#### `style_guide_files`
233	Crawled style guide files from the NAS. Agent sends raw data; server derives path metadata on ingest.
234	- `id uuid PK`
235	- `root_label text` — scan root name (e.g. `styleguides-nas`)
236	- `relative_path text NOT NULL` — path relative to scan root
237	- `filename text NOT NULL`
238	- `size_bytes bigint`, `modified_at timestamptz`, `quick_hash text`
239	- `licensor_name text GENERATED` — `split_part(relative_path, '/', 1)`, stored, indexed
240	- `property_folder text NULL` — second path segment
241	- `style_guide_folder text NULL` — third path segment
242	- `normalized_style_guide_folder text NULL` — lowercased, non-alphanum stripped
243	- `path_segments text[]`, `directory_path text`, `depth int`
244	- `is_active boolean DEFAULT true` — false for files not present in latest crawl
245	- `thumbnail_url text NULL`, `thumbnail_error text NULL` — not populated (no thumbnail pipeline)
246	- `crawl_run_id uuid FK`
247	
248	Unique constraint: `(root_label, relative_path)`. Indexes: `licensor_name`, `property_folder`, `style_guide_folder`, `is_active`.
249	
250	#### `style_guide_folders` (view)
251	DISTINCT `licensor_name, property_folder` pairs for the sidebar tree. `security_invoker = true` so RLS applies. Used by the frontend to build the folder tree without hitting the PostgREST 1000-row default cap on `style_guide_files`.
252	
253	#### `tiff_optimization_queue`
254	Queue for TIFF compression jobs run by the Windows Agent.
255	- `id uuid PK`, `asset_id uuid FK assets`, `status queue_status`, `claimed_by text`, `claimed_at`, `completed_at`, `error_message text`
256	
257	---
258	
259	## 3) Additional Tables
260	
261	### 3.1 asset_tags
262	Normalized tag storage. The `assets.tags` text array is a denormalized copy maintained by trigger.
263	
264	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
265	- `asset_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES assets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`
266	- `tag text NOT NULL`
267	- `source text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'manual'` — `'manual'` or `'ai'`
268	- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
269	- `created_by uuid NULL`
270	- `UNIQUE(asset_id, tag)`
271	
272	Indexes: `idx_asset_tags_asset_id`, `idx_asset_tags_source`
273	
274	### 3.2 tiff_optimization_queue
275	Tracks TIFF files on the NAS that are candidates for compression optimization.
276	
277	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
278	- `relative_path text NOT NULL UNIQUE`
279	- `filename text NOT NULL`
280	- `file_size bigint NOT NULL`
281	- `file_modified_at timestamptz NOT NULL`
282	- `file_created_at timestamptz NULL`
283	- `compression_type text NULL` — `none`, `zip`, `lzw`, `packbits`, `jpeg`, `deflate`
284	- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'scanned'` — `scanned`, `queued_test`, `queued_process`, `processing`, `completed`, `failed`
285	- `mode text NULL` — `test` or `process`
286	- `new_file_size bigint NULL` — size after compression
287	- `new_filename text NULL`
288	- `new_file_modified_at timestamptz NULL`
289	- `new_file_created_at timestamptz NULL`
290	- `original_backed_up boolean DEFAULT false`
291	- `original_deleted boolean DEFAULT false`
292	- `error_message text NULL`
293	- `scan_session_id text NULL`
294	- `claimed_by text NULL` — agent_id of agent processing this file
295	- `claimed_at timestamptz NULL`
296	- `processed_at timestamptz NULL`
297	- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
298	
299	Indexes: `idx_tiff_opt_status`, `idx_tiff_opt_compression`
300	
301	### 3.3 hygiene_findings
302	File quality issues found during hygiene scans (embedded rasters, oversized layers, etc.).
303	
304	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
305	- `asset_id uuid NULL REFERENCES assets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`
306	- `relative_path text NOT NULL`
307	- `filename text NOT NULL`
308	- `check_type text NOT NULL` — `ai_embedded_raster`, `tiff_uncompressed`, `psd_oversized_layer`
309	- `severity text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'warning'` — `info`, `warning`, `critical`
310	- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open'` — `open`, `dismissed`, `resolved`
311	- `details jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'` — check-specific detail payload
312	- `scan_session_id text NULL`
313	- `found_by_agent text NULL`
314	- `found_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
315	- `reviewed_by uuid NULL`
316	- `reviewed_at timestamptz NULL`
317	- `review_notes text NULL`
318	- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
319	
320	Indexes: `idx_hygiene_findings_check_type`, `idx_hygiene_findings_status`, `idx_hygiene_findings_asset_id`, `idx_hygiene_findings_relative_path`
321	
322	Unique: `(relative_path, check_type) WHERE status != 'resolved'` — prevents duplicate open findings for the same file + check type.
323	
324	### 3.4 erp_sync_runs
325	Job metadata for each ERP sync run.
326	
327	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
328	- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'running'` — `running`, `completed`, `failed`
329	- `started_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
330	- `ended_at timestamptz NULL`
331	- `total_fetched int DEFAULT 0`
332	- `total_upserted int DEFAULT 0`
333	- `total_errors int DEFAULT 0`
334	- `error_samples jsonb DEFAULT '[]'`
335	- `run_metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'`
336	- `created_by text NULL`
337	
338	### 3.5 erp_items_raw
339	Immutable audit snapshots of ERP API responses. One row per item per sync run. Never updated — append-only.
340	
341	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
342	- `external_id text NOT NULL` — ERP item ID
343	- `raw_payload jsonb NOT NULL` — full raw API response for this item
344	- `sync_run_id uuid NULL REFERENCES erp_sync_runs(id) ON DELETE SET NULL`
345	- `fetched_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
346	
347	Indexes: `idx_erp_items_raw_external_id`, `idx_erp_items_raw_sync_run_id`
348	
349	### 3.6 erp_items_current
350	Latest normalized row per ERP item (upserted on each sync). One row per `external_id`.
351	
352	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
353	- `external_id text UNIQUE NOT NULL`
354	- `style_number text NULL`
355	- `item_description text NULL`
356	- `mg_category text NULL` — top-level product category (NULL for legacy items pre-cutoff-date)
357	- `mg01_code` through `mg06_code text NULL` — merchandise group codes (6 tiers)
358	- `size_code text NULL`
359	- `licensor_code text NULL`
360	- `property_code text NULL`
361	- `division_code text NULL`
362	- `erp_updated_at timestamptz NULL` — timestamp from the ERP system
363	- `synced_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
364	- `sync_run_id uuid NULL REFERENCES erp_sync_runs(id) ON DELETE SET NULL`
365	- `source_system text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'designflow'`
366	- `raw_mg_fields jsonb DEFAULT '{}'` — raw MG field values before normalization
367	- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
368	- `updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
369	
370	Indexes: `idx_erp_items_current_style_number`, `idx_erp_items_current_mg_category`, `idx_erp_items_current_synced_at`
371	
372	### 3.7 product_category_predictions
373	AI classification results for ERP items that couldn't be categorized deterministically.
374	
375	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
376	- `erp_item_id uuid NULL REFERENCES erp_items_current(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`
377	- `external_id text NOT NULL`
378	- `predicted_category text NOT NULL` — `Wall`, `Tabletop`, `Clock`, `Storage`, `Workspace`, `Floor`, `Garden`
379	- `confidence real NOT NULL` — 0.0 to 1.0
380	- `rationale text NULL` — AI explanation of the classification
381	- `classification_source text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ai'`
382	- `ai_model text NULL` — model identifier used
383	- `ai_prompt_version text NULL`
384	- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'` — `pending`, `low_confidence`, `auto_applied`, `approved`, `rejected`, `unclassifiable`
385	- `reviewed_by uuid NULL`
386	- `reviewed_at timestamptz NULL`
387	- `input_context jsonb NULL` — context passed to AI for audit
388	- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
389	
390	Status lifecycle:
391	- `pending` — newly classified, awaiting review
392	- `low_confidence` — confidence < 0.65, needs human review
393	- `auto_applied` — confidence ≥ 0.65, automatically applied to assets
394	- `approved` — manually approved by admin
395	- `rejected` — manually rejected; item will be re-classified or left uncategorized
396	- `unclassifiable` — AI determined it cannot be classified
397	
398	Indexes: `idx_pcp_status`, `idx_pcp_erp_item_id`
399	
400	### 3.8 agent_pairings
401	One-time pairing codes for Bootstrap registration of new agents.
402	
403	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
404	- `pairing_code text NOT NULL UNIQUE`
405	- `agent_type text NOT NULL CHECK (agent_type IN ('bridge', 'windows-render'))`
406	- `agent_name text NOT NULL DEFAULT ''`
407	- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'consumed', 'expired'))`
408	- `created_by uuid NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id)`
409	- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
410	- `expires_at timestamptz NOT NULL`
411	- `consumed_at timestamptz NULL`
412	- `consumed_by_agent_id uuid NULL`
413	- `agent_registration_id uuid NULL REFERENCES agent_registrations(id)`
414	
415	Indexes: `idx_agent_pairings_code WHERE status = 'pending'`
416	
417	Pairing codes are short-lived (default 15 minutes). A new agent reads the code from its `.env`, calls `POST /agent/bootstrap`, and the code is consumed. The generate-install-bundle action creates a pairing code automatically and embeds it in the downloaded config files.
418	
419	### 3.9 app_access
420	Per-user entitlement for each app (`popdam` or `styleguides`). Controls which UI the user can access after login.
421	
422	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
423	- `user_id uuid NOT NULL`
424	- `app app_name NOT NULL` — `'popdam'` or `'styleguides'`
425	- `granted_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
426	- `granted_by uuid NULL`
427	- `UNIQUE (user_id, app)`
428	
429	RLS: users read their own rows (for app-switcher logic); admins manage all rows. Helper: `has_app_access(user_id, app_name) → bool`. New users get `app_access` rows from `handle_new_user()` based on the `invitations.apps` array.
430	
431	### 3.10 helper_devices
432	One row per desktop device that has the POP DAM Helper installed. Created/updated via `POST /helper-api/register-device`.
433	
434	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
435	- `user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id)`
436	- `device_name text NOT NULL`
437	- `device_os text NOT NULL CHECK (device_os IN ('windows', 'macos'))`
438	- `helper_version text NOT NULL`
439	- `last_seen_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
440	- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
441	
442	### 3.11 helper_tokens
443	Short-lived one-time tokens embedded in `popdam://` URLs. Generated by the web DAM, consumed by the Helper on first use.
444	
445	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
446	- `token text NOT NULL UNIQUE` — 32-char random hex
447	- `user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id)`
448	- `action text NOT NULL CHECK (action IN ('checkout', 'checkin', 'open', 'reveal', 'discard'))`
449	- `asset_id uuid NULL REFERENCES assets(id)`
450	- `checkout_id uuid NULL REFERENCES asset_checkouts(id)`
451	- `expires_at timestamptz NOT NULL` — 5 minutes from creation
452	- `consumed_at timestamptz NULL`
453	- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
454	
455	### 3.12 asset_checkouts
456	Tracks the lifecycle of a file checked out to a local machine. The unique partial index enforces one active checkout per asset.
457	
458	- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
459	- `asset_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES assets(id)`
460	- `user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id)`
461	- `device_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES helper_devices(id)`
462	- `status checkout_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'`
463	- `local_path text NULL`
464	- `snapshot_hash text NULL` — SHA-256 at checkout time
465	- `final_hash text NULL` — SHA-256 of the checked-in file
466	- `file_size bigint NULL`
467	- `last_heartbeat_at timestamptz NULL`
468	- `error_message text NULL`
469	- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
470	- `updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()` — trigger-maintained
471	
472	**`checkout_status` enum:** `active` → `checkin_queued` → `uploading` → `verifying` → `complete` (or `discarded` / `error` / `conflict` at any stage)
473	
474	**Unique partial index:** `UNIQUE (asset_id) WHERE status IN ('active', 'checkin_queued', 'uploading', 'verifying')` — the `/checkouts/start` handler catches `23505` (unique violation) and returns 409 with the existing owner's info.
475	
476	---
477	
478	## 4) Indexes (Performance-Critical)
479	- btree: `assets(file_type)`, `assets(status)`, `assets(workflow_status)`,
480	  `assets(is_licensed)`, `assets(modified_at)`, `assets(file_created_at)`,
481	  `assets(licensor_id)`, `assets(property_id)`, `assets(product_subtype_id)`,
482	  `assets(quick_hash)`
483	- GIN: `assets(tags)`
484	- trigram (pg_trgm): `assets(filename)` and optionally `assets(relative_path)`
485	
486	---
487	
488	## 4) Visibility Logic (Centralized and Consistent)
489	**Visibility is NOT dependent on having a Property or Character link.**
490	
491	Visible if ANY is true:
492	- `file_created_at >= THUMBNAIL_MIN_DATE`
493	- `modified_at >= THUMBNAIL_MIN_DATE`
494	- `thumbnail_url IS NOT NULL`
495	
496	Create a single SQL function (or view) used everywhere:
497	- main asset list query
498	- filter counts
499	- total counts
500	
501	---
502	
503	## 5) Required Functions / Triggers (High Level)
504	- `has_role(user_id, role)` for RLS
505	- `handle_new_user()` trigger: invitation-only enforcement
506	- queue functions: `claim_jobs(...)` using `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, `reset_stale_jobs(...)`
507	- optional: `get_filter_counts(filters)` and `get_asset_count(filters)` using the same visibility logic
508	
509	---
510	
511	## 6) RLS (Row-Level Security) Summary
512	- Frontend uses RLS-protected access.
513	- Agents operate through edge functions using service role inside the function.
514	- Policies must support:
515	  - authenticated users read visible assets
516	  - admins manage config/invitations
517	
518	Uniqueness Guard: Add a UNIQUE constraint on the pair (share_id, relative_path). This is the ultimate defense against duplicate assets.
519	
520	Soft Delete: Add is_deleted boolean DEFAULT false. This allows the Admin to "hide" unwanted folders from the UI without wiping their metadata history.
521	
522	
523	Audit Logs: Add last_scanned_at to the assets table to track which files are still "alive" on the disk during a scan.
524	
525	---
526	
527	## 7) Golden Rule: File Date Preservation
528	`modified_at` and `file_created_at` are filesystem-sourced timestamps. The DAM must NEVER cause these to change on the source file. The Bridge Agent must record original timestamps before touching a file and restore them after. If restoration fails, processing must halt. See PROJECT_BIBLE.md §15 for full details.
529

file.content:
# DATABASE SCHEMA (Postgres / Supabase)

This document is the single source of truth for tables, columns, constraints, indexes, and visibility rules.

Key principle:
- The DB must enforce correctness (especially timestamps and required fields) so the system fails loudly instead of silently drifting.

> **Triggers and functions** are documented in `docs/INFRASTRUCTURE.md`.

---
## Timestamp Audit (Recommended)
Store the timestamps the worker observed on disk during ingest:
- `modified_at` and `file_created_at` (already required)
Additionally recommended fields for audit/debug:
- `last_stat_observed_at` (when the worker last checked timestamps)
- `original_mtime_at_processing` / `original_birthtime_at_processing` (optional)
If a timestamp mutation incident occurs, record it in a `worker_incidents` table or an `asset_processing_events` log.


## 1) Enums (Create First)
- `file_type`: `psd`, `ai`
- `asset_status`: `pending`, `processing`, `tagged`, `error`
- `queue_status`: `pending`, `claimed`, `processing`, `completed`, `failed`
- `asset_type`: `art_piece`, `product`
- `art_source`: `freelancer`, `straight_style_guide`, `style_guide_composition`
- `workflow_status`:
  `product_ideas`, `concept_approved`, `in_development`, `freelancer_art`,
  `discontinued`, `in_process`, `customer_adopted`, `licensor_approved`, `other`
- `app_name`: `popdam`, `styleguides` — used by `app_access` and `invitations.apps`
- `app_role`: `admin`, `user`

---

## 2) Core Tables

### 2.1 licensors
- `id uuid PK`
- `name text NOT NULL`
- `external_id text UNIQUE NULL`
- `created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`
- `updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`

### 2.2 properties
- `id uuid PK`
- `licensor_id uuid FK NOT NULL`
- `name text NOT NULL`
- `external_id text UNIQUE NULL`
- timestamps

### 2.3 characters
- `id uuid PK`
- `property_id uuid FK NOT NULL`
- `name text NOT NULL`
- `external_id text UNIQUE NULL`
- timestamps

### 2.4 product_categories / product_types / product_subtypes
As in the build spec (taxonomy tables with optional external_id).

### 2.5 assets (main)
Canonical path storage uses `relative_path` (see PATH_UTILS.md).

Required columns:
- `id uuid PK`
- `filename text NOT NULL`
- `relative_path text NOT NULL`  (canonical, POSIX relative)
- `file_type file_type NOT NULL`
- `file_size bigint DEFAULT 0`
- `width int DEFAULT 0`
- `height int DEFAULT 0`
- `artboards int DEFAULT 1`
- `thumbnail_url text NULL` (full public URL)
- `thumbnail_error text NULL`
- `is_licensed boolean DEFAULT false`
- `licensor_id uuid FK NULL`
- `property_id uuid FK NULL`
- `product_subtype_id uuid FK NULL`
- `asset_type asset_type NULL`
- `art_source art_source NULL`
- `big_theme text NULL`
- `little_theme text NULL`
- `design_ref text NULL`
- `design_style text NULL`
- `ai_description text NULL`
- `scene_description text NULL`
- `tags text[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::text[]`
- `workflow_status workflow_status DEFAULT 'other'`
- `status asset_status DEFAULT 'pending'`
- Visibility Guard: Add is_deleted boolean DEFAULT false.
- Integrity Guard: Add UNIQUE(share_id, relative_path) to the assets table to prevent duplicate ingestion.

Hashing + scan bookkeeping:
- `quick_hash text NOT NULL`
- `quick_hash_version int NOT NULL DEFAULT 1`
- `last_seen_at timestamptz NOT NULL`
- `modified_at timestamptz NOT NULL`  **NO DEFAULT** (must be supplied by agent from disk)
- `file_created_at timestamptz NULL`  (agent supplies birthtime if available else = modified_at)
- `ingested_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`
- `created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`

Designer metadata (extracted by AI from Tech Pack thumbnails):
- `designer_name text NULL`
- `technical_designer_name text NULL`
- `freelancer_name text NULL`

Hard constraints:
- `modified_at` must be NOT NULL and have no default.
- `relative_path` must be NOT NULL.

### 2.6 asset_characters (join table)
- `asset_id uuid FK ON DELETE CASCADE`
- `character_id uuid FK`
- `UNIQUE(asset_id, character_id)`

### 2.7 asset_path_history
- `id uuid PK`
- `asset_id uuid FK`
- `old_relative_path text`
- `new_relative_path text`
- `detected_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`

### 2.8 processing_queue
- `id uuid PK`
- `asset_id uuid FK`
- `job_type text`
- `status queue_status DEFAULT 'pending'`
- `agent_id text NULL`
- `claimed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `completed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `error_message text NULL`

### 2.9 render_queue (optional windows render)
- `id uuid PK`
- `asset_id uuid FK`
- `status queue_status DEFAULT 'pending'`
- `claimed_by text NULL`
- `claimed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `completed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `error_message text NULL`

### 2.10 style_groups
- `id uuid PK`
- `sku text NOT NULL`
- `folder_path text NOT NULL`
- `primary_asset_id uuid FK NULL`
- `primary_asset_type text NULL` (mirrors selected primary asset `asset_type` for direct filtering)
- `asset_count int DEFAULT 0`
- `latest_file_date timestamptz NULL`
- `workflow_status workflow_status DEFAULT 'other'`
- licensing + taxonomy summary fields (licensor/property/category/division/MG/size)
- Designer rollup (from member assets, with conflict detection):
  - `designer_name text NULL`
  - `technical_designer_name text NULL`
  - `freelancer_name text NULL`
  - `designer_conflict boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false` (true when member assets have differing designer names)

### 2.11 agent_registrations
- `id uuid PK`
- `agent_name text NOT NULL`
- `agent_key_hash text UNIQUE NOT NULL`
- `last_heartbeat timestamptz`
- `metadata jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'`

### 2.12 profiles / user_roles / invitations
Invitation-only access model:
- profiles: `user_id uuid UNIQUE`, `email text`, `full_name text`, timestamps
- user_roles: `user_id uuid`, `role text`, `UNIQUE(user_id, role)`
- invitations: `id uuid PK`, `email text UNIQUE NOT NULL`, `role text DEFAULT 'user'`, `invited_by uuid NULL`, `created_at`, `accepted_at NULL`

### 2.13 admin_config
- `key text PK`
- `value jsonb NOT NULL`
- `updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()`
- `updated_by uuid NULL`

Stores:
- `THUMBNAIL_MIN_DATE`
- `SCAN_MIN_DATE`
- `SPACES_CONFIG` — DigitalOcean Spaces non-secret config (bucket, region, endpoint, public_base_url)
- `BULK_OPERATIONS` — state for all bulk jobs (see `docs/BULK_JOBS.md`)
- `SCAN_REQUEST` — pending scan trigger flags for agents
- `ERP_LAST_SYNC_DATE` — watermark for incremental ERP sync
- `ERP_SYNC_ENDPOINT` — override ERP API URL
- `ERP_CATEGORY_CUTOFF_DATE` — items before this date have mg_category nulled (legacy)
- taxonomy endpoint URLs
- NAS mapping keys (host/ip/share/mount root)
- AI tagging custom instructions

### 2.14 ERP Tables

#### `erp_sync_runs`
Metadata per sync execution.
- `id uuid PK`, `status text` (running/completed/failed), `started_at`, `ended_at`
- `total_fetched int`, `total_upserted int`, `total_errors int`, `error_samples jsonb`
- `run_metadata jsonb` (sync_mode, start_date, end_date), `created_by text`

#### `erp_items_current`
Latest normalized row per ERP item. Upserted on each sync.
- `id uuid PK`, `external_id text UNIQUE NOT NULL`
- `style_number text`, `item_description text`
- `mg_category text` — category from ERP API ("Wall", "Storage", etc.) — may be null
- `mg01_code text`, `mg02_code text`, `mg03_code text` — **single-character letter/digit codes** resolved from the API
- `mg04_code`, `mg05_code`, `mg06_code` — additional MG levels (rarely populated)
- `size_code`, `licensor_code`, `property_code`, `division_code`, `prepack_code`, `prepack_codes text[]`
- `erp_updated_at timestamptz` — ERP's own last-modified (used as "Created Date" in the browser)
- `synced_at timestamptz`, `sync_run_id uuid FK erp_sync_runs`
- `raw_mg_fields jsonb` — original API values: `mg01`/`mg02`/`mg03` (may be descriptions or codes), plus `mg01_code`/`mg02_code`/`mg03_code` (resolved) and other metadata fields
- `dismissed boolean DEFAULT false` — soft-hide from browser

#### `erp_items_raw`
Immutable snapshot of every ERP API response row.
- `id uuid PK`, `external_id text`, `raw_payload jsonb`, `sync_run_id uuid FK`, `fetched_at timestamptz`

#### `product_category_predictions`
AI classification results for ERP items missing `mg_category`.
- `id uuid PK`, `external_id text`, `erp_item_id uuid FK erp_items_current`
- `predicted_category text`, `confidence real`, `rationale text`
- `classification_source text` (erp/rule/ai), `ai_model text`
- `status text` (pending/auto_applied/approved/rejected/unclassifiable)
- `reviewed_by uuid`, `reviewed_at timestamptz`, `input_context jsonb`

### 2.15 Hygiene & Style Guide Tables

#### `hygiene_findings`
File naming/structure issues found during Windows Agent scans.
- `id uuid PK`, `scan_session_id uuid`, `file_path text`, `finding_type text`, `detail jsonb`, `found_at timestamptz`

#### `style_guide_crawl_runs`
Metadata for each licensor style guide crawl run.
- `id uuid PK`, `status text`, `agent_id text`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, `files_found int`, `error_message text`, `inaccessible_roots text[] NULL`

#### `style_guide_files`
Crawled style guide files from the NAS. Agent sends raw data; server derives path metadata on ingest.
- `id uuid PK`
- `root_label text` — scan root name (e.g. `styleguides-nas`)
- `relative_path text NOT NULL` — path relative to scan root
- `filename text NOT NULL`
- `size_bytes bigint`, `modified_at timestamptz`, `quick_hash text`
- `licensor_name text GENERATED` — `split_part(relative_path, '/', 1)`, stored, indexed
- `property_folder text NULL` — second path segment
- `style_guide_folder text NULL` — third path segment
- `normalized_style_guide_folder text NULL` — lowercased, non-alphanum stripped
- `path_segments text[]`, `directory_path text`, `depth int`
- `is_active boolean DEFAULT true` — false for files not present in latest crawl
- `thumbnail_url text NULL`, `thumbnail_error text NULL` — not populated (no thumbnail pipeline)
- `crawl_run_id uuid FK`

Unique constraint: `(root_label, relative_path)`. Indexes: `licensor_name`, `property_folder`, `style_guide_folder`, `is_active`.

#### `style_guide_folders` (view)
DISTINCT `licensor_name, property_folder` pairs for the sidebar tree. `security_invoker = true` so RLS applies. Used by the frontend to build the folder tree without hitting the PostgREST 1000-row default cap on `style_guide_files`.

#### `tiff_optimization_queue`
Queue for TIFF compression jobs run by the Windows Agent.
- `id uuid PK`, `asset_id uuid FK assets`, `status queue_status`, `claimed_by text`, `claimed_at`, `completed_at`, `error_message text`

---

## 3) Additional Tables

### 3.1 asset_tags
Normalized tag storage. The `assets.tags` text array is a denormalized copy maintained by trigger.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `asset_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES assets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`
- `tag text NOT NULL`
- `source text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'manual'` — `'manual'` or `'ai'`
- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `created_by uuid NULL`
- `UNIQUE(asset_id, tag)`

Indexes: `idx_asset_tags_asset_id`, `idx_asset_tags_source`

### 3.2 tiff_optimization_queue
Tracks TIFF files on the NAS that are candidates for compression optimization.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `relative_path text NOT NULL UNIQUE`
- `filename text NOT NULL`
- `file_size bigint NOT NULL`
- `file_modified_at timestamptz NOT NULL`
- `file_created_at timestamptz NULL`
- `compression_type text NULL` — `none`, `zip`, `lzw`, `packbits`, `jpeg`, `deflate`
- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'scanned'` — `scanned`, `queued_test`, `queued_process`, `processing`, `completed`, `failed`
- `mode text NULL` — `test` or `process`
- `new_file_size bigint NULL` — size after compression
- `new_filename text NULL`
- `new_file_modified_at timestamptz NULL`
- `new_file_created_at timestamptz NULL`
- `original_backed_up boolean DEFAULT false`
- `original_deleted boolean DEFAULT false`
- `error_message text NULL`
- `scan_session_id text NULL`
- `claimed_by text NULL` — agent_id of agent processing this file
- `claimed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `processed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`

Indexes: `idx_tiff_opt_status`, `idx_tiff_opt_compression`

### 3.3 hygiene_findings
File quality issues found during hygiene scans (embedded rasters, oversized layers, etc.).

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `asset_id uuid NULL REFERENCES assets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`
- `relative_path text NOT NULL`
- `filename text NOT NULL`
- `check_type text NOT NULL` — `ai_embedded_raster`, `tiff_uncompressed`, `psd_oversized_layer`
- `severity text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'warning'` — `info`, `warning`, `critical`
- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open'` — `open`, `dismissed`, `resolved`
- `details jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'` — check-specific detail payload
- `scan_session_id text NULL`
- `found_by_agent text NULL`
- `found_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `reviewed_by uuid NULL`
- `reviewed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `review_notes text NULL`
- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`

Indexes: `idx_hygiene_findings_check_type`, `idx_hygiene_findings_status`, `idx_hygiene_findings_asset_id`, `idx_hygiene_findings_relative_path`

Unique: `(relative_path, check_type) WHERE status != 'resolved'` — prevents duplicate open findings for the same file + check type.

### 3.4 erp_sync_runs
Job metadata for each ERP sync run.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'running'` — `running`, `completed`, `failed`
- `started_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `ended_at timestamptz NULL`
- `total_fetched int DEFAULT 0`
- `total_upserted int DEFAULT 0`
- `total_errors int DEFAULT 0`
- `error_samples jsonb DEFAULT '[]'`
- `run_metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'`
- `created_by text NULL`

### 3.5 erp_items_raw
Immutable audit snapshots of ERP API responses. One row per item per sync run. Never updated — append-only.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `external_id text NOT NULL` — ERP item ID
- `raw_payload jsonb NOT NULL` — full raw API response for this item
- `sync_run_id uuid NULL REFERENCES erp_sync_runs(id) ON DELETE SET NULL`
- `fetched_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`

Indexes: `idx_erp_items_raw_external_id`, `idx_erp_items_raw_sync_run_id`

### 3.6 erp_items_current
Latest normalized row per ERP item (upserted on each sync). One row per `external_id`.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `external_id text UNIQUE NOT NULL`
- `style_number text NULL`
- `item_description text NULL`
- `mg_category text NULL` — top-level product category (NULL for legacy items pre-cutoff-date)
- `mg01_code` through `mg06_code text NULL` — merchandise group codes (6 tiers)
- `size_code text NULL`
- `licensor_code text NULL`
- `property_code text NULL`
- `division_code text NULL`
- `erp_updated_at timestamptz NULL` — timestamp from the ERP system
- `synced_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `sync_run_id uuid NULL REFERENCES erp_sync_runs(id) ON DELETE SET NULL`
- `source_system text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'designflow'`
- `raw_mg_fields jsonb DEFAULT '{}'` — raw MG field values before normalization
- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`

Indexes: `idx_erp_items_current_style_number`, `idx_erp_items_current_mg_category`, `idx_erp_items_current_synced_at`

### 3.7 product_category_predictions
AI classification results for ERP items that couldn't be categorized deterministically.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `erp_item_id uuid NULL REFERENCES erp_items_current(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`
- `external_id text NOT NULL`
- `predicted_category text NOT NULL` — `Wall`, `Tabletop`, `Clock`, `Storage`, `Workspace`, `Floor`, `Garden`
- `confidence real NOT NULL` — 0.0 to 1.0
- `rationale text NULL` — AI explanation of the classification
- `classification_source text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ai'`
- `ai_model text NULL` — model identifier used
- `ai_prompt_version text NULL`
- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'` — `pending`, `low_confidence`, `auto_applied`, `approved`, `rejected`, `unclassifiable`
- `reviewed_by uuid NULL`
- `reviewed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `input_context jsonb NULL` — context passed to AI for audit
- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`

Status lifecycle:
- `pending` — newly classified, awaiting review
- `low_confidence` — confidence < 0.65, needs human review
- `auto_applied` — confidence ≥ 0.65, automatically applied to assets
- `approved` — manually approved by admin
- `rejected` — manually rejected; item will be re-classified or left uncategorized
- `unclassifiable` — AI determined it cannot be classified

Indexes: `idx_pcp_status`, `idx_pcp_erp_item_id`

### 3.8 agent_pairings
One-time pairing codes for Bootstrap registration of new agents.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `pairing_code text NOT NULL UNIQUE`
- `agent_type text NOT NULL CHECK (agent_type IN ('bridge', 'windows-render'))`
- `agent_name text NOT NULL DEFAULT ''`
- `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'consumed', 'expired'))`
- `created_by uuid NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id)`
- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `expires_at timestamptz NOT NULL`
- `consumed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `consumed_by_agent_id uuid NULL`
- `agent_registration_id uuid NULL REFERENCES agent_registrations(id)`

Indexes: `idx_agent_pairings_code WHERE status = 'pending'`

Pairing codes are short-lived (default 15 minutes). A new agent reads the code from its `.env`, calls `POST /agent/bootstrap`, and the code is consumed. The generate-install-bundle action creates a pairing code automatically and embeds it in the downloaded config files.

### 3.9 app_access
Per-user entitlement for each app (`popdam` or `styleguides`). Controls which UI the user can access after login.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `user_id uuid NOT NULL`
- `app app_name NOT NULL` — `'popdam'` or `'styleguides'`
- `granted_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `granted_by uuid NULL`
- `UNIQUE (user_id, app)`

RLS: users read their own rows (for app-switcher logic); admins manage all rows. Helper: `has_app_access(user_id, app_name) → bool`. New users get `app_access` rows from `handle_new_user()` based on the `invitations.apps` array.

### 3.10 helper_devices
One row per desktop device that has the POP DAM Helper installed. Created/updated via `POST /helper-api/register-device`.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id)`
- `device_name text NOT NULL`
- `device_os text NOT NULL CHECK (device_os IN ('windows', 'macos'))`
- `helper_version text NOT NULL`
- `last_seen_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`

### 3.11 helper_tokens
Short-lived one-time tokens embedded in `popdam://` URLs. Generated by the web DAM, consumed by the Helper on first use.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `token text NOT NULL UNIQUE` — 32-char random hex
- `user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id)`
- `action text NOT NULL CHECK (action IN ('checkout', 'checkin', 'open', 'reveal', 'discard'))`
- `asset_id uuid NULL REFERENCES assets(id)`
- `checkout_id uuid NULL REFERENCES asset_checkouts(id)`
- `expires_at timestamptz NOT NULL` — 5 minutes from creation
- `consumed_at timestamptz NULL`
- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`

### 3.12 asset_checkouts
Tracks the lifecycle of a file checked out to a local machine. The unique partial index enforces one active checkout per asset.

- `id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- `asset_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES assets(id)`
- `user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id)`
- `device_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES helper_devices(id)`
- `status checkout_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'`
- `local_path text NULL`
- `snapshot_hash text NULL` — SHA-256 at checkout time
- `final_hash text NULL` — SHA-256 of the checked-in file
- `file_size bigint NULL`
- `last_heartbeat_at timestamptz NULL`
- `error_message text NULL`
- `created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()` — trigger-maintained

**`checkout_status` enum:** `active` → `checkin_queued` → `uploading` → `verifying` → `complete` (or `discarded` / `error` / `conflict` at any stage)

**Unique partial index:** `UNIQUE (asset_id) WHERE status IN ('active', 'checkin_queued', 'uploading', 'verifying')` — the `/checkouts/start` handler catches `23505` (unique violation) and returns 409 with the existing owner's info.

---

## 4) Indexes (Performance-Critical)
- btree: `assets(file_type)`, `assets(status)`, `assets(workflow_status)`,
  `assets(is_licensed)`, `assets(modified_at)`, `assets(file_created_at)`,
  `assets(licensor_id)`, `assets(property_id)`, `assets(product_subtype_id)`,
  `assets(quick_hash)`
- GIN: `assets(tags)`
- trigram (pg_trgm): `assets(filename)` and optionally `assets(relative_path)`

---

## 4) Visibility Logic (Centralized and Consistent)
**Visibility is NOT dependent on having a Property or Character link.**

Visible if ANY is true:
- `file_created_at >= THUMBNAIL_MIN_DATE`
- `modified_at >= THUMBNAIL_MIN_DATE`
- `thumbnail_url IS NOT NULL`

Create a single SQL function (or view) used everywhere:
- main asset list query
- filter counts
- total counts

---

## 5) Required Functions / Triggers (High Level)
- `has_role(user_id, role)` for RLS
- `handle_new_user()` trigger: invitation-only enforcement
- queue functions: `claim_jobs(...)` using `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, `reset_stale_jobs(...)`
- optional: `get_filter_counts(filters)` and `get_asset_count(filters)` using the same visibility logic

---

## 6) RLS (Row-Level Security) Summary
- Frontend uses RLS-protected access.
- Agents operate through edge functions using service role inside the function.
- Policies must support:
  - authenticated users read visible assets
  - admins manage config/invitations

Uniqueness Guard: Add a UNIQUE constraint on the pair (share_id, relative_path). This is the ultimate defense against duplicate assets.

Soft Delete: Add is_deleted boolean DEFAULT false. This allows the Admin to "hide" unwanted folders from the UI without wiping their metadata history.


Audit Logs: Add last_scanned_at to the assets table to track which files are still "alive" on the disk during a scan.

---

## 7) Golden Rule: File Date Preservation
`modified_at` and `file_created_at` are filesystem-sourced timestamps. The DAM must NEVER cause these to change on the source file. The Bridge Agent must record original timestamps before touching a file and restore them after. If restoration fails, processing must halt. See PROJECT_BIBLE.md §15 for full details.

input.description: Map folder-path handling in codebase
input.prompt:
I'm working in the popdam3 repo at /worksp/popdam. I need to understand how file/folder paths are stored and used, so I can add logic that infers a "customer", "program", and "stage" from the folder a file sits in.

Please investigate and report back (with file:line references and short code excerpts):

1. The `assets` table schema — which columns store the file's NAS path / relative path / folder. Look in supabase/migrations and docs/SCHEMA.md. What is the canonical path column name(s)?

2. The `style_groups` table — how do groups relate to folders/paths? Do they have a path column?

3. How the bridge-agent scanner (apps/bridge-agent/src/scanner.ts) determines paths and what fields it writes to assets when ingesting. Show the relevant path fields.

4. The PopDAM filter sidebar in src/components/library/ — how filters are defined and applied (e.g. how a filter like "category" or "tag" is wired from UI → query). I want to know the pattern for adding a new filterable attribute.

5. How search works in the library — where the search query is built and what columns it searches.

6. Any existing notion of "folder", "program", "customer", "stage", "collection", "license", or "brand" already in the schema or code.

7. docs/PATH_UTILS.md path canonicalization rules — summarize how paths are normalized.

Report the concrete column names, the relevant files, and the patterns for adding a new filterable+searchable attribute. Be thorough but return only conclusions and key excerpts, not full file dumps.

lastPrompt: read the .md files and remove anything from your context window that does not help you solve this problem: the users want the system to associate a file with: a customer a customer 'Program' based on…

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings:

## PopDAM Path & Schema Investigation Report

### 1. ASSETS TABLE SCHEMA — Path Storage
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/supabase/migrations/20260220125350_1b4ac457-e648-499a-a8e7-a6f6e01eb542.sql`

**Canonical path column:** `relative_path text NOT NULL`
- Stores POSIX-style paths (forward slashes, no leading/trailing slash)
- Example: `Decor/Projects/Foo/bar.psd`
- Unique constraint enforced: `UNIQUE (relative_path)`
- No path segments are stored in separate columns for inference

**Assets table columns relevant to categorization** (added via `/worksp/popdam/supabase/migrations/20260224172609_0c88425a-0bf9-459f-a07d-af5461884415.sql`):
```sql
-- ERP enrichment fields (NOT inferred from path):
product_category TEXT          -- Top-level category (Wall, Storage, etc.)
mg01_code, mg02_code, mg03_code TEXT  -- Merchandise group codes (6 tiers)
mg04_code, mg05_code, mg06_code TEXT
licensor_code TEXT, licensor_name TEXT
property_code TEXT, property_name TEXT
division_code TEXT, division_name TEXT
size_code TEXT, size_name TEXT
sku TEXT                       -- SKU extracted from folder path
```

**Key insight:** There is NO `customer`, `program`, `stage`, `collection`, `license`, or `brand` field in assets currently. These would need to be added.

### 2. STYLE_GROUPS TABLE — Path & Grouping
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/supabase/migrations/20260224200816_4c2c4b03-dd19-47f8-8b24-154bd930b31d.sql`

**Structure:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE public.style_groups (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  folder_path TEXT NOT NULL,  -- Path up to and including the SKU segment
  primary_asset_id UUID,
  asset_count INT,
  -- ERP fields copied from representative asset:
  product_category TEXT,
  licensor_code TEXT, licensor_name TEXT,
  property_code TEXT, property_name TEXT,
  division_code TEXT, division_name TEXT,
  mg01_code, mg02_code, mg03_code TEXT (+ names),
  size_code, size_name TEXT,
  workflow_status,
  is_licensed BOOLEAN
);
```

**Key insight:** `style_groups` has `folder_path` (the directory containing all assets for that SKU group). This is NOT the path-to-extract-metadata-from; it's a grouping artifact. The SKU is extracted from the first path segment matching the pattern `^[A-Za-z]{1,6}[0-9]` with length >= 10.

### 3. BRIDGE AGENT SCANNER — Path Determination
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/apps/bridge-agent/src/scanner.ts` (lines 230–249)

**How paths are written:**
```typescript
// Build canonical relative path (POSIX, no leading slash) per PATH_UTILS.md §1
const effectiveRoot = mountRoot || config.nasContainerMountRoot;
let relPath = relative(effectiveRoot, fullPath);
relPath = relPath.split("\\").join("/"); // Ensure POSIX
if (relPath.startsWith("/")) relPath = relPath.slice(1);

yield {
  relativePath: relPath,          // e.g. "Decor/Projects/SKU1234567890/file.psd"
  filename: basename(entry.name), // e.g. "file.psd"
  fileType: "psd" | "ai" | "pdf",
  modifiedAt: s.mtime,
  fileCreatedAt: s.birthtime || null,
  // ... size, stat info
};
```

**Scanner writes NO segments/metadata to database—only the full `relative_path`.** Path inference happens downstream in ERP enrichment or grouping logic.

### 4. POPDAM FILTER SIDEBAR — Pattern for Adding Filters
**Files:**
- `/worksp/popdam/src/types/assets.ts` (type definition)
- `/worksp/popdam/src/components/library/FilterSidebar.tsx` (UI)
- `/worksp/popdam/src/hooks/useAssets.ts` (query builder)

**Current filter structure** (lines 12–25 in assets.ts):
```typescript
export interface AssetFilters {
  search: string;
  fileType: string[];           // .psd, .ai, .pdf
  status: string[];             // pending, processing, tagged, error
  workflowStatus: string[];
  isLicensed: boolean | null;
  licensorId: string | null;    // FK-based filter
  propertyId: string | null;    // FK-based filter
  assetType: string[];          // art_piece, product, packaging, tech_pack, photography
  artSource: string[];          // freelancer, straight_style_guide, ...
  tagFilter: string;
  fileStatus: FileStatusFilter[];
  productCategory: string[];    // ERP category filter (NOT path-inferred)
}
```

**Pattern to add a new filterable attribute (e.g., `customer`):**

1. **Schema:** Add column to `assets` table
   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN customer TEXT;
   ```

2. **Type definition** (`/worksp/popdam/src/types/assets.ts`):
   ```typescript
   interface AssetFilters {
     // ... existing fields
     customer: string | null;  // or string[] if multi-select
   }
   
   const defaultFilters: AssetFilters = {
     // ... existing defaults
     customer: null,
   };
   ```

3. **Query builder** (`/worksp/popdam/src/hooks/useAssets.ts`, function `applyFilters`):
   ```typescript
   if (filters.customer) {
     query = query.eq("customer", filters.customer);
   }
   ```

4. **UI sidebar** (`/worksp/popdam/src/components/library/FilterSidebar.tsx`):
   ```typescript
   <SearchableCombo
     label="Customer"
     options={/* fetch from useFilterOptions or hardcode */}
     value={filters.customer}
     onChange={(val) => onFiltersChange({ ...filters, customer: val })}
   />
   ```

5. **RPC for filter counts** (call `get_filter_counts` with new field in payload):
   ```typescript
   const filterPayload: Record<string, unknown> = {
     // ... existing
     customer: filters.customer,
   };
   ```

### 5. SEARCH IMPLEMENTATION
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/src/hooks/useAssets.ts` (lines 29–86)

**Current search mechanism:**
```typescript
function applyFilters(query: any, filters: AssetFilters) {
  if (filters.search) {
    query = query.ilike("filename", `%${filters.search}%`);
  }
  // ... other filters
  return query;
}
```

**Key limitations:**
- Search **only searches `filename`** (case-insensitive substring match via `ilike`)
- Does **NOT search `relative_path`** — only filename
- Uses Supabase PostgREST `.ilike()` operator (basic LIKE pattern)

**To add path search or full-text search:**
1. Add trigram index: `CREATE INDEX idx_assets_relative_path_trgm ON assets USING gin (relative_path gin_trgm_ops);`
2. Modify query builder to also search `relative_path`:
   ```typescript
   if (filters.search) {
     query = query.or(
       `filename.ilike.%${filters.search}%,relative_path.ilike.%${filters.search}%`
     );
   }
   ```

### 6. EXISTING METADATA NOTIONS IN SCHEMA
**Already in `assets` table:**
- `is_licensed` boolean — licensed vs. generic
- `asset_type` enum: `art_piece`, `product`, `packaging`, `tech_pack`, `photography`
- `art_source` enum: `freelancer`, `straight_style_guide`, `style_guide_composition`
- `workflow_status` enum: `product_ideas`, `concept_approved`, `in_development`, `freelancer_art`, `discontinued`, `in_process`, `customer_adopted`, `licensor_approved`, `other`
- `product_category` text — ERP-sourced (Wall, Storage, etc.)
- `tags` text[] — user/AI-added tags

**NOT in schema:**
- `customer`, `program`, `stage`, `collection`, `license`, `brand` — **must be added**

### 7. PATH_UTILS.MD — Canonicalization Rules
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/docs/PATH_UTILS.md`

**Storage (DB):**
- Stored as normalized POSIX relative path: `relative_path` column
- No leading slash, no trailing slash
- Forward slashes only
- Collapse repeated separators
- Example: `Decor/Projects/Foo/bar.psd`

**Display modes (derived, not stored):**
```
Office UNC (hostname):     \\{NAS_HOST}\{NAS_SHARE}\Decor\Projects\Foo\bar.psd
Office UNC (IP):           \\{NAS_IP}\{NAS_SHARE}\Decor\Projects\Foo\bar.psd
Remote (Synology Drive):   {USER_SYNC_ROOT}\{NAS_SHARE}\Decor\Projects\Foo\bar.psd
Container (Bridge Agent):  {NAS_CONTAINER_MOUNT_ROOT}/Decor/Projects/Foo/bar.psd
```

**Parsing rules:**
- Normalize backslash → forward slash
- Collapse `//` to `/`
- Remove leading/trailing `/`
- Reject `..` traversal
- Case-insensitive share matching
- Treat `USER_SYNC_ROOT` as literal prefix (no drive letter assumptions)

---

## Summary for Adding Path-Based Metadata Inference

**To infer `customer`, `program`, `stage` from folder paths:**

1. **Define the path structure rule** (e.g., `customer/program/stage/SKU/file.psd`)

2. **Add columns to assets table:**
   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN customer TEXT;
   ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN program TEXT;
   ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN stage TEXT;
   ```

3. **Parse in bridge-agent scanner** (after building `relativePath`, before inserting):
   ```typescript
   const segments = relPath.split("/");
   const customer = segments[0] ?? null;
   const program = segments[1] ?? null;
   const stage = segments[2] ?? null;
   // Then pass to ingest payload
   ```

4. **Backfill existing assets** via migration using SQL string_to_array:
   ```sql
   UPDATE assets SET
     customer = (string_to_array(relative_path, '/'))[1],
     program = (string_to_array(relative_path, '/'))[2],
     stage = (string_to_array(relative_path, '/'))[3]
   WHERE is_deleted = false;
   ```

5. **Wire filters into UI** following the pattern in section 4 above

6. **Index for performance:**
   ```sql
   CREATE INDEX idx_assets_customer ON assets (customer);
   CREATE INDEX idx_assets_program ON assets (program);
   CREATE INDEX idx_assets_stage ON assets (stage);
   ```

I'm working in the popdam3 repo at /worksp/popdam. I need to understand how file/folder paths are stored and used, so I can add logic that infers a "customer", "program", and "stage" from the folder a file sits in.

Please investigate and report back (with file:line references and short code excerpts):

1. The `assets` table schema — which columns store the file's NAS path / relative path / folder. Look in supabase/migrations and docs/SCHEMA.md. What is the canonical path column name(s)?

2. The `style_groups` table — how do groups relate to folders/paths? Do they have a path column?

3. How the bridge-agent scanner (apps/bridge-agent/src/scanner.ts) determines paths and what fields it writes to assets when ingesting. Show the relevant path fields.

4. The PopDAM filter sidebar in src/components/library/ — how filters are defined and applied (e.g. how a filter like "category" or "tag" is wired from UI → query). I want to know the pattern for adding a new filterable attribute.

5. How search works in the library — where the search query is built and what columns it searches.

6. Any existing notion of "folder", "program", "customer", "stage", "collection", "license", or "brand" already in the schema or code.

7. docs/PATH_UTILS.md path canonicalization rules — summarize how paths are normalized.

Report the concrete column names, the relevant files, and the patterns for adding a new filterable+searchable attribute. Be thorough but return only conclusions and key excerpts, not full file dumps.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings:

## PopDAM Path & Schema Investigation Report

### 1. ASSETS TABLE SCHEMA — Path Storage
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/supabase/migrations/20260220125350_1b4ac457-e648-499a-a8e7-a6f6e01eb542.sql`

**Canonical path column:** `relative_path text NOT NULL`
- Stores POSIX-style paths (forward slashes, no leading/trailing slash)
- Example: `Decor/Projects/Foo/bar.psd`
- Unique constraint enforced: `UNIQUE (relative_path)`
- No path segments are stored in separate columns for inference

**Assets table columns relevant to categorization** (added via `/worksp/popdam/supabase/migrations/20260224172609_0c88425a-0bf9-459f-a07d-af5461884415.sql`):
```sql
-- ERP enrichment fields (NOT inferred from path):
product_category TEXT          -- Top-level category (Wall, Storage, etc.)
mg01_code, mg02_code, mg03_code TEXT  -- Merchandise group codes (6 tiers)
mg04_code, mg05_code, mg06_code TEXT
licensor_code TEXT, licensor_name TEXT
property_code TEXT, property_name TEXT
division_code TEXT, division_name TEXT
size_code TEXT, size_name TEXT
sku TEXT                       -- SKU extracted from folder path
```

**Key insight:** There is NO `customer`, `program`, `stage`, `collection`, `license`, or `brand` field in assets currently. These would need to be added.

### 2. STYLE_GROUPS TABLE — Path & Grouping
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/supabase/migrations/20260224200816_4c2c4b03-dd19-47f8-8b24-154bd930b31d.sql`

**Structure:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE public.style_groups (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  folder_path TEXT NOT NULL,  -- Path up to and including the SKU segment
  primary_asset_id UUID,
  asset_count INT,
  -- ERP fields copied from representative asset:
  product_category TEXT,
  licensor_code TEXT, licensor_name TEXT,
  property_code TEXT, property_name TEXT,
  division_code TEXT, division_name TEXT,
  mg01_code, mg02_code, mg03_code TEXT (+ names),
  size_code, size_name TEXT,
  workflow_status,
  is_licensed BOOLEAN
);
```

**Key insight:** `style_groups` has `folder_path` (the directory containing all assets for that SKU group). This is NOT the path-to-extract-metadata-from; it's a grouping artifact. The SKU is extracted from the first path segment matching the pattern `^[A-Za-z]{1,6}[0-9]` with length >= 10.

### 3. BRIDGE AGENT SCANNER — Path Determination
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/apps/bridge-agent/src/scanner.ts` (lines 230–249)

**How paths are written:**
```typescript
// Build canonical relative path (POSIX, no leading slash) per PATH_UTILS.md §1
const effectiveRoot = mountRoot || config.nasContainerMountRoot;
let relPath = relative(effectiveRoot, fullPath);
relPath = relPath.split("\\").join("/"); // Ensure POSIX
if (relPath.startsWith("/")) relPath = relPath.slice(1);

yield {
  relativePath: relPath,          // e.g. "Decor/Projects/SKU1234567890/file.psd"
  filename: basename(entry.name), // e.g. "file.psd"
  fileType: "psd" | "ai" | "pdf",
  modifiedAt: s.mtime,
  fileCreatedAt: s.birthtime || null,
  // ... size, stat info
};
```

**Scanner writes NO segments/metadata to database—only the full `relative_path`.** Path inference happens downstream in ERP enrichment or grouping logic.

### 4. POPDAM FILTER SIDEBAR — Pattern for Adding Filters
**Files:**
- `/worksp/popdam/src/types/assets.ts` (type definition)
- `/worksp/popdam/src/components/library/FilterSidebar.tsx` (UI)
- `/worksp/popdam/src/hooks/useAssets.ts` (query builder)

**Current filter structure** (lines 12–25 in assets.ts):
```typescript
export interface AssetFilters {
  search: string;
  fileType: string[];           // .psd, .ai, .pdf
  status: string[];             // pending, processing, tagged, error
  workflowStatus: string[];
  isLicensed: boolean | null;
  licensorId: string | null;    // FK-based filter
  propertyId: string | null;    // FK-based filter
  assetType: string[];          // art_piece, product, packaging, tech_pack, photography
  artSource: string[];          // freelancer, straight_style_guide, ...
  tagFilter: string;
  fileStatus: FileStatusFilter[];
  productCategory: string[];    // ERP category filter (NOT path-inferred)
}
```

**Pattern to add a new filterable attribute (e.g., `customer`):**

1. **Schema:** Add column to `assets` table
   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN customer TEXT;
   ```

2. **Type definition** (`/worksp/popdam/src/types/assets.ts`):
   ```typescript
   interface AssetFilters {
     // ... existing fields
     customer: string | null;  // or string[] if multi-select
   }
   
   const defaultFilters: AssetFilters = {
     // ... existing defaults
     customer: null,
   };
   ```

3. **Query builder** (`/worksp/popdam/src/hooks/useAssets.ts`, function `applyFilters`):
   ```typescript
   if (filters.customer) {
     query = query.eq("customer", filters.customer);
   }
   ```

4. **UI sidebar** (`/worksp/popdam/src/components/library/FilterSidebar.tsx`):
   ```typescript
   <SearchableCombo
     label="Customer"
     options={/* fetch from useFilterOptions or hardcode */}
     value={filters.customer}
     onChange={(val) => onFiltersChange({ ...filters, customer: val })}
   />
   ```

5. **RPC for filter counts** (call `get_filter_counts` with new field in payload):
   ```typescript
   const filterPayload: Record<string, unknown> = {
     // ... existing
     customer: filters.customer,
   };
   ```

### 5. SEARCH IMPLEMENTATION
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/src/hooks/useAssets.ts` (lines 29–86)

**Current search mechanism:**
```typescript
function applyFilters(query: any, filters: AssetFilters) {
  if (filters.search) {
    query = query.ilike("filename", `%${filters.search}%`);
  }
  // ... other filters
  return query;
}
```

**Key limitations:**
- Search **only searches `filename`** (case-insensitive substring match via `ilike`)
- Does **NOT search `relative_path`** — only filename
- Uses Supabase PostgREST `.ilike()` operator (basic LIKE pattern)

**To add path search or full-text search:**
1. Add trigram index: `CREATE INDEX idx_assets_relative_path_trgm ON assets USING gin (relative_path gin_trgm_ops);`
2. Modify query builder to also search `relative_path`:
   ```typescript
   if (filters.search) {
     query = query.or(
       `filename.ilike.%${filters.search}%,relative_path.ilike.%${filters.search}%`
     );
   }
   ```

### 6. EXISTING METADATA NOTIONS IN SCHEMA
**Already in `assets` table:**
- `is_licensed` boolean — licensed vs. generic
- `asset_type` enum: `art_piece`, `product`, `packaging`, `tech_pack`, `photography`
- `art_source` enum: `freelancer`, `straight_style_guide`, `style_guide_composition`
- `workflow_status` enum: `product_ideas`, `concept_approved`, `in_development`, `freelancer_art`, `discontinued`, `in_process`, `customer_adopted`, `licensor_approved`, `other`
- `product_category` text — ERP-sourced (Wall, Storage, etc.)
- `tags` text[] — user/AI-added tags

**NOT in schema:**
- `customer`, `program`, `stage`, `collection`, `license`, `brand` — **must be added**

### 7. PATH_UTILS.MD — Canonicalization Rules
**File:** `/worksp/popdam/docs/PATH_UTILS.md`

**Storage (DB):**
- Stored as normalized POSIX relative path: `relative_path` column
- No leading slash, no trailing slash
- Forward slashes only
- Collapse repeated separators
- Example: `Decor/Projects/Foo/bar.psd`

**Display modes (derived, not stored):**
```
Office UNC (hostname):     \\{NAS_HOST}\{NAS_SHARE}\Decor\Projects\Foo\bar.psd
Office UNC (IP):           \\{NAS_IP}\{NAS_SHARE}\Decor\Projects\Foo\bar.psd
Remote (Synology Drive):   {USER_SYNC_ROOT}\{NAS_SHARE}\Decor\Projects\Foo\bar.psd
Container (Bridge Agent):  {NAS_CONTAINER_MOUNT_ROOT}/Decor/Projects/Foo/bar.psd
```

**Parsing rules:**
- Normalize backslash → forward slash
- Collapse `//` to `/`
- Remove leading/trailing `/`
- Reject `..` traversal
- Case-insensitive share matching
- Treat `USER_SYNC_ROOT` as literal prefix (no drive letter assumptions)

---

## Summary for Adding Path-Based Metadata Inference

**To infer `customer`, `program`, `stage` from folder paths:**

1. **Define the path structure rule** (e.g., `customer/program/stage/SKU/file.psd`)

2. **Add columns to assets table:**
   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN customer TEXT;
   ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN program TEXT;
   ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN stage TEXT;
   ```

3. **Parse in bridge-agent scanner** (after building `relativePath`, before inserting):
   ```typescript
   const segments = relPath.split("/");
   const customer = segments[0] ?? null;
   const program = segments[1] ?? null;
   const stage = segments[2] ?? null;
   // Then pass to ingest payload
   ```

4. **Backfill existing assets** via migration using SQL string_to_array:
   ```sql
   UPDATE assets SET
     customer = (string_to_array(relative_path, '/'))[1],
     program = (string_to_array(relative_path, '/'))[2],
     stage = (string_to_array(relative_path, '/'))[3]
   WHERE is_deleted = false;
   ```

5. **Wire filters into UI** following the pattern in section 4 above

6. **Index for performance:**
   ```sql
   CREATE INDEX idx_assets_customer ON assets (customer);
   CREATE INDEX idx_assets_program ON assets (program);
   CREATE INDEX idx_assets_stage ON assets (stage);
   ```

I have the schema and code patterns. The critical missing piece is the **actual folder layout on the NAS** — I can't write reliable inference rules without seeing how real paths are structured (where stage/customer/program actually sit relative to the SKU like `CAC62DCJK01`). content.text:
Now the picture is complete and it explains the user's deferral note:

- **In Development → Customer Adopted → `<Customer>` → `<Program>` → `<SKU>`** — this branch is customer/program organized (Ross, Ross Wall 2026). 34,746 files.
- **Concept Approved Designs → `<PRODUCT TYPE>`** (WALL ART, TABLETOP, STORAGE…) — organized by product type, *not* customer/program. This is precisely why the user said those subfolders need mapping "later."

Let me confirm the customer/program level under "Customer Adopted" and check the other "In Development" buckets.

input.query:
SELECT split_part(relative_path,'/',6) AS customer, count(*) files, count(DISTINCT split_part(relative_path,'/',7)) programs
FROM assets WHERE is_deleted=false
  AND relative_path LIKE 'Decor/Character Licensed/____New Structure/In Development/Customer Adopted/%'
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 40;

content.text: `_FINISHED` is a wrapper bucket: `_FINISHED / <Customer>_finished / <Program> / <SKU>`. So inference needs to handle a couple of variants, plus status buckets (`_NOT APPROVED`, `_REJECTED`, etc.). I have enough on the data. Now let me ground the implementation in the actual filter/search code and the filter-count RPC.

lastPrompt: read the .md files and remove anything from your context window that does not help you solve this problem: the users want the system to associate a file with: a customer a customer 'Program' based on…

content.text:
I now have everything I need to design this. The structure is:

```
Decor / Character Licensed / ____New Structure / <STAGE> / ... / <Customer> / <Program> / <SKU> / files
```

- **Stage** (reliable for *all* files): the folder right under `____New Structure` — `In Development`, `Concept Approved Designs`, `Product Ideas`, `Freelancer art`, `Discontinued`.
- **Customer + Program** (reliable only in the `In Development → Customer Adopted` branch right now): e.g. `Ross` / `Ross Wall 2026`. Two layouts: direct (`Customer/Program/SKU`) and the `_FINISHED/<Customer>_finished/Program/SKU` wrapper. The `Concept Approved Designs` tree is organized by product type, not customer — which is exactly what you flagged for "later."

Since inference is purely a function of the path, I'll do it in **SQL** (one inference function + columns on `assets` and `style_groups`, a trigger for new ingests, and a backfill) — no agent redeploy, one source of truth. Before I build, two decisions that change the implementation:

questions.question: For v1, how wide should customer/program inference go? (Stage will be set for ALL files under ____New Structure regardless.)

options.label: Customer Adopted branch only
options.description: Fill customer+program only where the path reliably encodes them: In Development → Customer Adopted (incl. unwrapping _FINISHED/<Customer>_finished). Leave blank elsewhere (Concept Approved Designs, etc.) to map later. Cleanest, no wrong guesses.

options.label: Also attempt other branches
options.description: Additionally try to infer customer/program in Generic Decor → Customer Programs and similar. Broader coverage now, but more edge cases and risk of mislabeling.

options.label: New dedicated 'stage' column
options.description: Store the literal folder name (In Development, Concept Approved Designs, …) in a new stage column on assets and style_groups. Keeps it independent of the existing workflow_status logic. Recommended.

options.label: Reuse existing workflow_status
options.description: Map folder → existing workflow_status enum and overwrite it. Risk: collides with ERP/other logic that already writes workflow_status.

content.subject: Frontend: filters + search for new attributes
content.description: Add stage/customer/program to AssetFilters, useAssets + useStyleGroups query builders (filter + search), FilterSidebar UI (stage checkboxes, customer/program combos), usePathFacets hook, wire in Index.tsx, FacetCounts type.

content.subject: Display attributes in detail panels + commit/deploy
content.description: Show Stage/Customer/Program in AssetDetailPanel and StyleGroupDetailPanel. Lint, commit, push to main+github, verify CI for supabase + frontend.

lastPrompt: read the .md files and remove anything from your context window that does not help you solve this problem: the users want the system to associate a file with: a customer a customer 'Program' based on…

content.text:
The example resolves exactly as requested: `CAC62DCJK01` → **In Development / Ross / Ross Wall 2026**, and the `_FINISHED` wrapper correctly unwraps `Ross_finished` → `Ross`. 

Now the final DB piece: update `get_filter_counts` so the existing facet counts respect the new filters and emit a `stage` facet (powers the Stage checkbox counts).

input.content:
---
name: project_db_backfill_batching
description: Big single-statement backfills on popdam-prod time out at the MCP gateway and can crash the compute into WAL recovery — batch them
metadata:
  type: project
---

On popdam-prod (`ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb`), the `assets` table is ~100k+ rows. A single `UPDATE ... FROM (SELECT infer_path_attrs(...) FROM assets)` over the whole table:
- exceeds the Supabase MCP `execute_sql`/`apply_migration` gateway wall-clock limit (~2 min) → "Connection terminated unexpectedly", and
- a too-heavy batch (~55k rows in one statement) crashed the compute instance into crash recovery ("redo in progress", `57P03 the database system is not accepting connections`) for several minutes (2026-06-08, during the stage/customer/program backfill).

**How to apply:** Keep DDL in the `apply_migration` migration; run large data backfills separately via `execute_sql`, scoped into batches of ~20k rows (e.g. partition by a `LIKE` prefix or `id`-range / `LIMIT`). A statement that loses its client connection may still commit server-side — re-check row counts before re-running. See [[project_cicd]].

input.content:
---
name: project_git_root_ownership
description: In the /worksp/popdam workspace, .git and dist/ periodically revert to root ownership, breaking git/build as user 'ai' — fix with sudo chown
metadata:
  type: project
---

In the `/worksp/popdam` harness workspace, the agent runs as user `ai` but `.git/` (index + loose objects) and `dist/` are sometimes owned by `root`, causing `fatal: .git/index: index file open failed: Permission denied`, `unable to open loose object`, and vite `EACCES ... dist/assets`. It recurs mid-session (e.g. after a build invokes git).

**How to apply:** passwordless sudo is available. When git or build hits a permission error, run `sudo -n chown -R ai:ai .git` (and `sudo -n chown -R ai:ai dist` for build). This is a legitimate environment fix, not a workaround of the no-workarounds rule. Then proceed with the normal `git push origin main` + `git push github main` flow. Note: origin and github remotes both point at the same GitHub URL here, so the second push reports "Everything up-to-date".

content.text:
Done and deployed. Here's what shipped.

## What it does
Each file and style group now carries three folder-derived attributes, inferred from the NAS path under `Decor/Character Licensed/____New Structure/…`:

- **Stage** — the folder under `____New Structure` (`In Development`, `Concept Approved Designs`, `Product Ideas`, `Freelancer art`, `Discontinued`). Set on **every** file there.
- **Customer** + **Program** — from the `In Development → Customer Adopted` branch, including unwrapping the `_FINISHED/<Customer>_finished/<Program>` bucket. `_`-prefixed status buckets (`_NOT APPROVED`, `_REJECTED`, etc.) correctly resolve to no customer.

Your example resolves exactly: `CAC62DCJK01` → **In Development / Ross / Ross Wall 2026**.

## How it's wired
- **Inference lives in SQL** (`infer_path_attrs()`), so it's one source of truth. A `BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE` trigger auto-populates new ingests — no agent redeploy needed. All ~100k existing rows backfilled (verified: 0 missing stages; In Development = 36,908 with customer / 35,788 with program).
- **Filters**: Stage (multi-select), Customer, and Program now appear in the sidebar in both Groups and Assets views.
- **Search**: searching `Ross Wall 2026` now matches the program field on both files and groups, surfacing everything associated.
- **Detail panels**: Stage/Customer/Program show on the asset and style-group panels.

CI is green across all three workflows (Supabase deploy, frontend publish, CI).

## Notes
- **`Concept Approved Designs`** is organized by product type (WALL ART, TABLETOP…), not customer — so those files get a stage but no customer/program, ready for the subfolder-attribute mapping you mentioned doing later.
- One incident worth flagging: a large single-statement backfill briefly crashed the Supabase compute into WAL recovery (~few min downtime). It recovered fully and the data is intact; I've recorded the batching lesson so future backfills are chunked.
The remaining sleep timers were just for polling CI, which has already completed successfully. Everything is shipped and deployed — no further action needed.

That was the last leftover polling timer. The work is complete and live: stage/customer/program inferred for all files and groups, filters and search wired up, and all CI workflows green. Nothing left to do.
